---
title: "BNR Metric Specification Register"
subtitle: "Standing definitions for approved information hub metrics"
date: "2026-05-15"
version: "Draft v0.1"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

## Document control

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| Document name | BNR Metric Specification Register |
| Short name | Metric register |
| Version | Draft v0.1 |
| Date | 15 May 2026 |
| Owner | BNR Analytics Team |
| Companion document | [BNR Information Hub Statistical Analysis Plan](sap.qmd) |

## Purpose

This register defines approved BNR information hub metrics.

The metric system is the permanent BNR deliverable. Briefings are temporary interpretations that consume those metrics. A briefing may go beyond the defined core metrics where needed for research, interpretation or communication.

This register is designed to be expanded over time. The first version focuses on core cardiovascular disease metrics required for routine surveillance, public reporting and operational monitoring.

## How to read this register

Each metric has a stable identifier, a short definition, an equation, and a small set of required metadata.

| Item | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | Stable identifier, for example `CVD-RATE-001` |
| Status | Draft, approved, pilot, retired or internal |
| Metric type | Count, percentage, rate, ratio, distribution, descriptive summary or data quality metric |
| Numerator | What is counted in the top line of the calculation |
| Denominator | What is counted in the bottom line, where relevant |
| Unit of analysis | Event, person, admission, death, bed-day, record or population |
| Stratifications | Standard breakdowns such as period, event type, sex or age group |
| Output | Metric outputs should include the metric ID, release, period, stratum, statistic, value, unit, numerator, denominator, confidence interval where relevant, and status flag |

Stratifications are dimensions of a metric. They do not create separate metrics unless the definition or method changes.

For example, stroke counts and AMI counts are both stratifications of the hospital-registered CVD event count metric. They do not need separate metric definitions.

## CVD burden metrics

### CVD-BURDEN-001: Hospital-registered CVD event count

**Definition.**  
The number of eligible hospital-registered CVD events recorded by the BNR in a reporting period.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Count} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} I_i
$$

where \(I_i = 1\) when record \(i\) is an eligible CVD event, and \(I_i = 0\) otherwise.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-BURDEN-001 |
| Metric name | Hospital-registered CVD event count |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core |
| Plain-language description | The number of hospital-registered CVD events recorded by the BNR in a reporting period. |
| Metric type | Count |
| Numerator | Eligible hospital-registered CVD events |
| Denominator | Not applicable |
| Unit of analysis | Event |
| Population and exclusions | Barbados residents with confirmed hospital-registered CVD events; exclude non-residents, duplicates and records outside the reporting period. DCO-only records are excluded unless source status explicitly includes them. |
| Stratifications | Required: period, event type. Optional: sex, age group, source status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Suppress or flag low-count strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_burden.do` |
| Notes | Stroke and AMI are stratifications of this metric, not separate metrics. |

### CVD-BURDEN-002: CVD event distribution

**Definition.**  
The percentage distribution of CVD events across event type, sex, age group or another standard dimension.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Percentage} = \frac{x_c}{N_p} \times 100
$$

where \(x_c\) is the number of events in the selected category, and \(N_p\) is the number of events in the parent group.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-BURDEN-002 |
| Metric name | CVD event distribution |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Supporting |
| Plain-language description | The percentage distribution of CVD events across event type, sex, age group or another standard dimension. |
| Metric type | Distribution |
| Numerator | Eligible CVD events in the selected category |
| Denominator | Eligible CVD events in the parent group |
| Unit of analysis | Event |
| Population and exclusions | Same event set as CVD-BURDEN-001. |
| Stratifications | Required: period and selected distribution dimension. Optional: event type, sex, age group, source status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Suppress or flag low-count strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_burden.do` |
| Notes | Used for age, sex and event-type composition summaries. |

## CVD rate metrics

### CVD-RATE-001: Hospital-registered CVD event rate

**Definition.**  
The crude and directly age-adjusted rates of eligible hospital-registered CVD events relative to the resident population.

**Equations.**

Crude event rate:

$$
\text{Crude rate per 100,000} = \frac{x}{N} \times 100{,}000
$$

where \(x\) is the number of eligible CVD events, and \(N\) is the resident population denominator for the same period and stratum.

Age-adjusted event rate:

$$
\text{Age-adjusted rate per 100,000} = \sum_{a=1}^{A} w_a r_a
$$

where \(r_a\) is the age-specific event rate in age group \(a\), and \(w_a\) is the standard population weight for age group \(a\).

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-RATE-001 |
| Metric name | Hospital-registered CVD event rate |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core |
| Plain-language description | The crude event rate shows the number of hospital-registered CVD events relative to the resident population. The age-adjusted event rate applies standard age weights so that rates can be compared more fairly across groups or years with different age structures. |
| Metric type | Rate / age-adjusted rate |
| Numerator | Eligible hospital-registered CVD events in the reporting period; for age-adjusted rates, eligible events by age group |
| Denominator | Resident population for the same period and stratum; for age-adjusted rates, resident population by age group |
| Unit of analysis | Event and population |
| Population and exclusions | Barbados residents; exclude non-residents, duplicates and DCO-only records unless explicitly included. |
| Stratifications | Required: period, event type, rate type. Optional: sex, age group for crude age-specific rates, source status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | 95% confidence interval using Poisson or exact Poisson method for crude rates, and the approved direct standardisation method for age-adjusted rates. Suppress or flag low-count or unstable strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_rate.do` |
| Notes | Stroke and AMI rates are stratifications of this metric. Age adjustment will always use the UN World Population Prospects age distributions from the latest WPP release. The WPP release and standard age weights must be recorded in metadata. |

## CVD outcome metrics

### CVD-OUTCOME-001: In-hospital case fatality

**Definition.**  
The percentage of eligible hospital-registered CVD events where the patient died before discharge.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Case fatality} = \frac{d}{n} \times 100
$$

where \(d\) is the number of in-hospital deaths after eligible CVD events, and \(n\) is the number of eligible CVD events with known discharge outcome.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-OUTCOME-001 |
| Metric name | In-hospital case fatality |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core |
| Plain-language description | The percentage of hospital-registered CVD events where the patient died before discharge. |
| Metric type | Percentage |
| Numerator | Eligible events where the patient died before discharge |
| Denominator | Eligible hospital-registered events with known discharge outcome |
| Unit of analysis | Hospital-registered event |
| Population and exclusions | Hospital-registered CVD events among Barbados residents; exclude DCO-only records, non-residents, duplicates and records with unknown discharge outcome. |
| Stratifications | Required: period, event type. Optional: sex, age group. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | 95% binomial confidence interval for crude estimates. Model-based interval for age-adjusted estimates where used. Suppress or flag low-count strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_outcome.do` |
| Notes | Stroke and AMI case fatality are stratifications of this metric. |

## CVD hospital-use metrics

### CVD-HOSP-001: Length of stay after hospital-registered CVD event

**Definition.**  
The number of days spent in hospital after a registered CVD event.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Length of stay}_i = \text{date of discharge}_i - \text{date of admission}_i
$$

where \(i\) is an eligible hospital admission linked to a CVD event.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-HOSP-001 |
| Metric name | Length of stay after hospital-registered CVD event |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core |
| Plain-language description | The number of days spent in hospital after a registered CVD event. |
| Metric type | Descriptive summary |
| Numerator | Not applicable |
| Denominator | Eligible admissions with valid admission and discharge dates |
| Unit of analysis | Hospital admission linked to CVD event |
| Population and exclusions | Hospital-registered CVD events with valid admission and discharge dates; exclude DCO-only records, missing dates and implausible date sequences. |
| Stratifications | Required: period, event type. Optional: sex, age group, outcome status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Suppress or flag low-count strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_hosp.do` |
| Notes | Median and interquartile range are preferred for public interpretation. |

### CVD-HOSP-002: CVD hospital bed-days

**Definition.**  
The total number of hospital bed-days used by people admitted with registered CVD events.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Total bed-days} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} \text{length of stay}_i
$$

where \(n\) is the number of eligible admissions.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-HOSP-002 |
| Metric name | CVD hospital bed-days |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core for service planning |
| Plain-language description | The total number of hospital bed-days used by people admitted with registered CVD events. |
| Metric type | Count / service-use metric |
| Numerator | Sum of length of stay across eligible admissions |
| Denominator | Optional: eligible admissions, where reporting bed-days per event |
| Unit of analysis | Bed-day |
| Population and exclusions | Same event set as CVD-HOSP-001. |
| Stratifications | Required: period, event type. Optional: sex, age group, outcome status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Suppress or flag low-count strata according to disclosure rules. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_hosp.do` |
| Notes | This is a service-use metric, not an incidence metric. |

## CVD data quality metrics

### CVD-DQ-001: Completeness of key CVD variables

**Definition.**  
The percentage of eligible records with complete values for key variables needed for analysis.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Completeness} = \frac{c}{n} \times 100
$$

where \(c\) is the number of records with a non-missing value for the selected variable, and \(n\) is the number of records eligible for completeness assessment.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-DQ-001 |
| Metric name | Completeness of key CVD variables |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Core QA |
| Plain-language description | The percentage of eligible records with complete values for key variables needed for analysis. |
| Metric type | Data quality percentage |
| Numerator | Records with non-missing value for the selected variable |
| Denominator | Records eligible for completeness assessment |
| Unit of analysis | Record |
| Population and exclusions | Records in the approved release eligible for the assessed variable; exclude records where the variable is not applicable. |
| Stratifications | Required: release ID, variable name. Optional: event type, source status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Flag variables below agreed completeness thresholds. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_dq.do` |
| Notes | Key variables include event date, sex, age or date of birth, admission date, discharge date and discharge outcome. |

### CVD-DQ-002: Timeliness of abstraction

**Definition.**   
The time between event, discharge or source availability and completion of abstraction.

**Equation.**

$$
\text{Abstraction lag}_i = \text{date abstraction completed}_i - \text{reference start date}_i
$$

where \(i\) is an eligible record with valid abstraction timing fields.

| Field | Specification |
|---|---|
| Metric ID | CVD-DQ-002 |
| Metric name | Timeliness of abstraction |
| Status | Draft |
| Priority | Process monitoring |
| Plain-language description | The time between event, discharge or source availability and completion of abstraction. |
| Metric type | Process monitoring summary |
| Numerator | Not applicable |
| Denominator | Records with required abstraction timing fields |
| Unit of analysis | Record |
| Population and exclusions | Records with valid abstraction timing fields; exclude records missing required dates. |
| Stratifications | Required: release ID. Optional: event type, source status. |
| Uncertainty / suppression | No routine confidence interval. Flag values outside agreed operational targets. |
| Responsible code | `metric_cvd_dq.do` |
| Notes | Reference start date and target threshold to be confirmed by BNR operations. |

## Change history

| Version | Date | Summary | Author |
|---|---|---|---|
| Draft v0.1 | 2026-05-15 | Initial draft metric register for the BNR information hub |
